untranslatable_reason: This signal requires option-specific pricing analysis (theoretical option value progression, bid-ask spread measurement relative to mispricing, and spread valuation comparisons) which cannot be expressed using equity market indicators. The core logic involves detecting relative mispricings between related derivative instruments, volatility surface violations, and arbitrage opportunities that require options pricing models (Black-Scholes derivatives, implied vol surfaces, Greeks) not available in the allowed indicator set.
translation_notes: NATENBERG_DRY_110_0 describes a relative value/arbitrage trading strategy endemic to options markets. It requires: (1) calculation of theoretical option/spread prices, (2) comparison of market prices to theoretical values, (3) analysis of bid-ask spreads relative to identified mispricings, and (4) evaluation of time decay impact on positions. None of these can be approximated from equity market OHLCV data and technical indicators. This strategy is not translatable to a spot/futures backtest engine without options pricing infrastructure.
